data.title: US HHS to start study on cellphone radiation
data.description:
The U.S. Department of Health and Human Services will start a study on cellphone radiation, a department spokesman said on Thursday, Reuters reported.

The move builds on Secretary Robert F. Kennedy Jr.'s criticism linking them to neurological damage and cancer, the report added.

The HHS did not immediately respond to a request for comment from Seeking Alpha.

Last year, the department noted that 22 states had curbed cellphone use in schools to improve the mental and physical health of children under the "Make America Healthy Again" movement.

The U.S. Food and Drug Administration also took down old webpages saying cellphones are not dangerous.

"The FDA removed webpages with old conclusions about cell phone radiation while HHS undertakes a study on electromagnetic radiation and health research to identify gaps in knowledge, including on new technologies, to ensure safety and efficacy," said Andrew Nixon an HHS spokesperson.

"The study was directed by President Trump's MAHA Commission in its strategy report," Nixon noted.

However, some webpages of agencies like the U.S. Centers for Disease Control and Prevention say there is no scientific evidence that provides a definite answer to whether using a cell phone causes cancer. The CDC page noted that more research is needed before we know if using cell phones causes health effects.

data.title: Instacart is seen at risk from the expanded Uber-Kroger partnership
data.description:
Wedbush Securities warned that Maplebear (CART), which does business under the name Instacart, could be impacted negatively from Uber Technologies' (UBER) launch of an expanded partnership with Kroger (KR) across the platform. The rollout enables Uber (UBER) customers to shop ~2,700 stores across Kroger's (KR) family of brands directly through the Uber Eats app, including the Mariano's, Fred Meyer, Ralphs, and Harris Teeter chains. 

Notably, Uber Eats' restaurant selection will also be integrated directly on the Kroger (KR) app. The companies will also deepen their relationship to innovate on retail media offerings for brands.

Analyst Scott Devitt said the expanded Uber (UBER)-Kroger (KR) partnership directly challenges Instacart's (CART) position among intermediaries. "In our view, Instacart is at risk of losing incremental market share to other leading intermediaries and grocers that can leverage their scale and success in adjacent categories to capture demand for online grocery and convenience," he noted.

While Instacart (CART) will still power Kroger's (KR) same-day delivery service, Devitt and his team think the expanded (UBER)-Kroger (KR)  partnership further validates the firm's thesis that Instacart's (CART) leading position within online grocery will erode over time as Amazon (AMZN) and others grab market share. 

Wedbush has an Underperform rating on Instacart (CART) and a price target of $36. Shares of CART are down 11.5% for the early part of 2026.

data.title: Mount Logan Capital prices $40 million senior notes offering
data.description:
• Mount Logan Capital (MLCI) has priced its previously announced underwritten public offering of $40 million in aggregate principal amount of 8.00% senior unsecured notes due 2031.
  • The notes will be issued in denominations of $25 and integral multiples of $25 in excess thereof and will bear interest at 8.00% per year, payable quarterly, with the first interest payment occurring on April 30, 2026.
  • The company has granted the underwriters a 30-day option to purchase up to an additional $6.0 million aggregate principal amount of notes to cover overallotments, if any.
  • The offering is expected to close on January 26, 2026.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Cloudflare rebounds as investors digest deal for Human Native
data.description:
Cloudflare (NET) shares rebounded on Friday, rising more than 1% in premarket trading, as investors digested the announcement of its intent to acquire Human Native.

On Thursday, shares of the internet infrastructure company fell more than 2% after it said it would acquire Human Native, which bills itself as an artificial intelligence data marketplace. The deal would further expand Cloudflare into the realm of transactions between AI developers and content creators, the company said in a statement.

“Content creators deserve full control over their work, whether they want to write for humans or optimize for AI,” Cloudflare CEO and co-founder Matthew Prince said in the release. “Last year we gave publishers and creators the tools to control which bots can access their content, but the real goal has always been to help create a new economic model that actually works for the next phase of the Internet. The Human Native team will help us accelerate the next phase, where we build the tools that allow content to be discovered, priced, and purchased through transparent channels. This acquisition is about building the tools needed to protect the longevity of the open Internet.”

Human Native was founded in 2024 and is backed by some of London's top venture capitalists. It has worked with companies in the tech and media spaces, including Google's (GOOG) (GOOGL) DeepMind, Figma (FIG), Bloomberg and others.

“We started Human Native with the goal of getting Generative AI out of its Napster era. We believe that creators should have control, compensation and credit when their work is used to power AI products, ensuring equitable compensation for rights holders while enabling responsible AI development,” Dr. James Smith, co-founder & CEO of Human Native, added. “Our discussions with Cloudflare, from Matthew to the entire team, have shown us that our values could not be more aligned, we believe in the same mission and together we will have the opportunity to deliver solutions at Internet scale.”

Financial terms of the deal were not disclosed, and Cloudflare did not say when the acquisition would close.

data.title: ImmunityBio shares climb as bladder cancer trial nears full enrollment, BLA submission expected by year-end
data.description:
• ImmunityBio (IBRX) on Friday said that enrollment in its registrational QUILT-2.005 trial for BCG-naïve non-muscle-invasive bladder cancer has surpassed expectations, with more than 85% of patients enrolled and full enrollment expected by Q2 2026.
  • The company's shares rose 23% premarket to $4.88.
  • Based on the current enrollment trajectory, ImmunityBio anticipates submitting a biologics license application (BLA) to the U.S. Food and Drug Administration by year's end, it said in a statement.
  • ImmunityBio (IBRX) said an interim analysis requested by the FDA demonstrated a statistically significant longer duration of complete response with ANKTIVA + BCG.

data.title: Mosaic shares tumble on weak preliminary Q4 results
data.description:
• The Mosaic Company (MOS) on Friday that a challenging fertilizer market is expected to weigh on its fourth quarter performance with phosphate sales volumes at ~1.3 million tonnes and potash sales volumes at ~2.2 million tonnes.
  • In the fourth quarter, to respond to weaker demand, Mosaic adjusted its phosphate production plan and product mix and redirected products to markets with stronger demand, resulting in production volumes that remained consistent with the prior quarter, the company said.
  • Challenging market conditions also weighed on cash flow, with fourth-quarter results pressured by lower sales and the resulting inventory build, it added.
  • Mosaic Fertilizantes' Brazil sales volumes fell meaningfully short of expectations. Full-year sales volumes were ~9 million tonnes, flat year over year, but reflective of the broader market downturn.
  • However, the company has guided for a stronger 2026, with global phosphate and potash shipments expected to reach record levels in the year. 
  • MOS -7.18% premarket to $25.6.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: AST SpaceMobile stock jumps on securing prime contract position on MDA’s SHIELD program
data.description:
• AST SpaceMobile (ASTS) jumped over 8% premarket on Friday after securing a prime contract position on the U.S. Missile Defense Agency SHIELD Program.
  • The Scalable Homeland Innovative Enterprise Layered Defense ((SHIELD)) program is part of the broader Golden Dome strategy, focused on building resilient, layered protection against air, missile, space, cyber, and hybrid threats from all operational domains.
  • The award was made as part of a list of companies eligible to compete under the program, publicly announced by the U.S. Government on January 15, 2026.
  • “Being selected as a prime contract awardee for the MDA’s SHIELD program is a major validation of our unique, on-orbit, dual-use technology and our growing capabilities within the defense sector,” said Chris Ivory, chief commercial officer and head of government business of AST SpaceMobile.
  • ASTS +8.12% premarket to $109.47
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: PNC Financial Q4 earnings top consensus, driven by record NII, fee income
data.description:
PNC Financial Services (PNC) stock climbed 3.7% in Friday premarket trading after the Pittsburgh-based bank delivered better-than-expected Q1 earnings, helped by strong net interest income and fee growth, and issued strong 2026 and Q1 guidance.

The bank expects full-year 2026 net interest income growth of ~14% from 2025’s $14.4B. That implies 2026 NII of ~$16.4B vs. the Visible Alpha consensus of $16.1B. Revenue is expected to grow ~11% from 2025's $23.1B, implying total revenue of ~$25.6B vs. $24.8B consensus. Noninterest costs, excluding one-time integration costs, are anticipated to increase ~7% from last year's $13.8B, or ~$14.8B (vs. Visible Alpha estimate of $14.7B).

Overall, the company targets generating ~400 basis points of positive operating leverage in 2026, it said.

For Q1, PNC (PNC) expects total revenue to increase 2%-3% from Q4's $6.1B, implying $6.2B-$6.3B (vs. $6.0B consensus), and net interest income is expected to grow ~6% from Q4 2025's $3.7B, implying $3.9B for the quarter (in line with the Visible Alpha consensus). 

The company expects to buy back about $600M-$700M of common stock in Q1 2026.

Q4 GAAP EPS of $4.88, vs. the average analyst estimate of $4.21, rose from $4.35 in Q3 and $3.77 in the prior year’s Q4.

Q4 revenue of $6.07B, topping the $5.95B consensus, increased from $5.92B in the prior quarter and $5.57B a year ago. Revenue growth rose 3% Q/Q, driven by records in net interest income and fee income.

Net interest income of $3.73B, vs. the Visible Alpha estimate of $3.70B, grew from $3.65B in Q3 and $3.52B in Q4 2024.

Fee income of $2.12B climbed 3% Q/Q and 14% Y/Y.

Provision for credit losses of $139M dropped from $167M in the previous quarter and $156M a year ago.

Noninterest expense of $3.60B vs. $3.46B in Q3 and $3.51B in Q4 a year ago.

Average loans grew to $327.9B from $325.9B in Q3. Average deposits of $439.5B, compared with $431.8B in the previous quarter.

After the close of Q4, PNC Financial (PNC) closed on its acquisition of FirstBank on Jan. 5, 2026.

Conference call at 9:00 AM ET.

data.title: Hershey to spend more on advertising its iconic chocolate brand - report
data.description:
Hershey (HSY) has increased the marketing budget for its 131-year-old chocolate brand by 20% to fund its first new advertising campaign in eight years, The Wall Street Journal reported.

The advertising push for the Hershey's brand will begin next week. Besides traditional and streaming TV, Hershey (HSY) will also use marketing channels where it has less experience, such as TikTok campaigns. 

It is also eyeing live events and activities around this year's big cultural moments, like the Winter Olympics and America250 festivities.

The chocolate brand will also be promoted in an upcoming biographical film called Hershey, based on the company's founder Milton Hershey and his wife Kitty.

Hershey (HSY) is also planning new iterations of the chocolate brand. "Across the whole portfolio, we are going to be ensuring that we have options that push the envelope a little more, deliver texture, deliver flavor, in a way that's new and unique," Stacy Taffet, chief growth officer at Hershey (HSY), told WSJ.

data.title: McDonald's plans to expand in Vietnam after becoming an aspirational brand
data.description:
McDonald's Corporation (MCD) is making its most ambitious push yet in Vietnam, with a plan to add about 100 outlets over the next three years. That would more than triple the restaurant company's current presence in Vietnam and signal a renewed commitment to a market where its growth has lagged rivals. 

The expansion drive in Vietnam by McDonald's (MCD) focuses on broadening geographic reach beyond core urban centers and repositioning the chain away from a premium image toward more everyday, value-oriented consumption in a country where chicken-focused brands like KFC (YUM) and Lotteria have long dominated.

The fast-food giant's Vietnamese story began with the 2014 opening of its first restaurant in Ho Chi Minh City, operated by developmental licensee Henry Nguyen, a U.S.-educated entrepreneur and son-in-law of then–Prime Minister Nguyen Tan Dung. The debut outlet generated intense curiosity as one of the few 24/7, drive-thru concepts in the country at the time and helped position McDonald's (MCD) as a modern, aspirational Western brand. 

McDonald's (MCD) currently operates restaurants in Ho Chi Minh City, Hanoi, Binh Duong, and Nha Trang, giving it a small but visible presence that can serve as a base for franchising and further rollouts.
